Rigetti Computing, a leading quantum computing startup and pioneer in hybrid quantum-classical computing systems, has announced [in a statement](http://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2020/08/04/2072699/0/en/Bessemer-Leads-79M-Series-C-Round-of-Rigetti-Computing-to-Advance-Practical-Quantum-Computing.html) that it closed a $79 million Series C financing led by [Bessemer Venture Partners.

interaction_phrase: founder and CEO of Rigetti Computing
occurrence_sentence: "This round of financing brings us one step closer to delivering quantum advantage to the market," said Chad Rigetti, founder and CEO of Rigetti Computing.

interaction_phrase: leads a $79 million Series C round in
occurrence_sentence: Bessemer Venture Partners leads a $79 million Series C round in quantum computing pioneer, Rigetti Computing.
In December 2019, Rigetti released a 32-qubit quantum computer that is currently available through Amazon Braket and its own Quantum Cloud Services platform.
In addition, the company recently received an $8.6 million DARPA award to build a quantum system capable of outperforming classical computers.

interaction_phrase: leads 79 million Series C round of Rigetti Computing
occurrence_sentence: Bessemer leads 79 million Series C round of Rigetti Computing to advance practical quantum computing.

"Quantum computing will drive a paradigm shift in high-performance computers as we continue pushing the boundaries of science deeper into the realms of science fiction," said Tomer Diari, a leading deep-tech investor from Bessemer Venture Partners who has joined the company's board.
In December 2019, Rigetti released a 32-qubit quantum computer that is currently available through Amazon Braket and its own Quantum Cloud Services platform.

interaction_phrase: one of a select few leaders
occurrence_sentence: "It's hard to find an area where quantum computing won't be tremendously valuable once quantum advantage is achieved," said Jonathan Curtis, vice president and portfolio manager at Franklin Equity Group.
